commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin, provider, channel, Codex app-server, SDK, and outbound message-action metadata readers against malformed or unreadable synthetic values with focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-local bug class: the PR body gives direct pre/post repros and focused tests, and the remaining review findings are source-reproducible from the PR head. I did not rerun tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This maintainer-labeled draft needs author/maintainer repair of the two helper blockers plus a compatibility decision and proof refresh before merge.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept test fixtures synthetic: current test content uses `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*` names rather than the reported third-party plugin name.
- Added follow-up hardening for outbound structured attachment params, outbound JSON params (`presentation`, `delivery`, `interactive`), `runMessageAction` input param copying, exported message action normalization/target-spec helpers, and exported message action threading controls.
- Bound message action params through shared guarded readers and a bounded null-prototype snapshot so unreadable key enumeration fails closed, unreadable route/control/target-alias/threading/idempotency params fail closed, `__proto__` route fields are not inherited, and oversized param bags reject instead of truncating.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, message action validation, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic message action threading controls: before fix raw `topLevel`/`threadId` getter reads threw; after fix unreadable threading controls become `ToolInputError` instead of raw errors or inherited reply/thread context.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic message action normalization params: before fix raw spread/accessor reads threw; after fix unreadable route params become `ToolInputError` and target probes return safely.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repro for unreadable synthetic message action runner params: before fix object spread threw a raw getter error; after fix unreadable payload-only params become absent and unreadable route/control params fail closed.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for unreadable synthetic provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 20 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (3 files / 84 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 37 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 34 tests after JSON param parser hardening)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 30 tests after structured attachment hardening)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/common.params.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/boolean-param.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 files / 26 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file / 14 tests)
- Earlier focused batches for manifest/tool availability, provider auth/onboarding, channel/config fixtures, Codex image payloads, and fuzz guard type/lint drift.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets.
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest local autoreview on the message action threading-control slice: clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Latest focused proof after rebase to `e12a6d6a67`: `message-action-runner.threading.test.ts` passed 1 file / 20 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- After that proof, `origin/main` advanced to `101cb70844` with zero touched-path overlap; the branch rebased cleanly and cheap post-rebase oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Current branch head after rebase/push: `8d0b5e30acc287d6b7233c68cafa1278b8e8b089`.
- Current PR base observed from GitHub: `101cb708444e637a965956b3e1f8728b043075c7`.
- Current test/source/docs scrub: no reported third-party plugin name remains in checked repo content for this branch.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Shared action/tool parameter readers turn unreadable required fields into normal input errors, treat unreadable optional fields as absent, fail unreadable action gates closed, and reject oversized string-array params instead of doing partial actions.
- Outbound structured attachment media hints copy by bounded index, skip unreadable entries, and reject oversized attachment arrays instead of crashing or partially truncating.
- Outbound JSON action params parse through guarded reads/writes so malformed plugin-provided accessors cannot throw before action normalization.
- Message action normalization now reuses the shared safe param snapshot; exported target checks no longer throw on unreadable target fields or plugin-param probes.
- Message action threading suppression now reads `topLevel`, `top_level`, `threadId`, and `thread_id` through guarded fail-closed accessors before applying implicit reply/thread behavior.
- Numeric legacy `to`/`channelId` values still fall back to current context, while numeric action aliases such as `chatId` remain valid target aliases.
- Provider wizard setup choices, gateway startup manifest planning, native approval routing, manifest tool availability, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema-quarantine fixtures retain their previously proven behavior.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- The latest broad Crabbox gate predates the newest outbound runner/normalization/threading slices; those slices have focused local proof and local autoreview.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid schema, provider hint, setup choice, channel allowlist, manifest alias, native approval route, Codex payload field, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, fail closed only for route/control/identity/idempotency-style action params in the newest outbound slices, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve numeric alias behavior, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates from earlier branch slices.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `8d0b5e30acc287d6b7233c68cafa1278b8e8b089`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed, including Codex URL-backed image-block preservation, provider wizard receiver handling, native approval false-positive target matching, shared param-reader silent truncation, message action `topLevel`/`idempotencyKey` fail-closed handling, numeric legacy-target preservation, and guarded message action threading controls.
target.author: vincentkoc
